He noted Alkermes had recently closed a $2.2B acquisition and is consolidating ERP systems (SAP, Oracle, JAGGAER to S/4HANA), which implies material addressable spend and complexity, but he did not translate that into a ROI case or cost avoidance number. champion.narrative: Dominic Ferrari is the identified champion, but his strength is weak and cooling. On the 11 May 2026 call he explicitly stated 'you have only one advocate, me' and recounted internal resistance when he attempted to socialize insights from the relevant stakeholder event with his broader team ('I tried to share what I learned at Horizon and got a lot of blank stares... people are heads-down on the ERP project'). His advocacy is genuine—he invited Zycus to present, requested the Gartner MQ and overview deck, and committed to confirming the Symphony expiration date—but his influence is limited: he does not have executive access (no relationship with CFO Ricky Levine or Head of Procurement Marcelo Loureiro visible in the record), he could not mobilize internal stakeholders to engage post-Horizon, and he has not responded to rep outreach in 55 days despite committing to follow-up actions on 11 May. His role is Senior Sourcing Analyst under VP Tom Harvey, which is an influencer position, not a decision-maker. The champion is cooling: zero contact since 11 May, no reply to materials sent, no progression on the two commitments he made (Symphony date, internal socialization). The risk is that Dominic becomes overwhelmed by the ERP project and de-prioritizes the Zycus evaluation, or that his advocacy is dismissed by senior stakeholders ('you have only one advocate') and the deal stalls. The move is immediate re-engagement by the deal owner (Richard Hunsinger) to check in on Dominic's bandwidth and co-develop a lightweight exec briefing he can use to escalate to Tom Harvey, plus an executive connect (Alexa Bradley) to Tom Harvey to build a second thread and validate the evaluation priority at the VP level.

competition.narrative: Alkermes is currently using Symphony (formerly Simfoni) for spend analytics and is approaching a contract renewal, estimated by Dominic Ferrari as 'possibly around December' 2026 though he was uncertain and has not confirmed the date. On the 11 May call Dominic described dissatisfaction with Symphony ('limited visibility into tail spend', 'not getting the insights we need'), signaling an active replacement evaluation rather than an incumbent-retention lean. He did not mention any other competitors by name—no Coupa, Ariba, GEP, Ivalua, or Jaggaer Spend Analytics references appeared in the call transcript, tasks, or Salesforce fields—so the competitive set is currently Symphony (status quo / do-nothing) versus Zycus. However, the evaluation has not formally started ('we haven't kicked off a formal evaluation yet'), and it is common in pharma S2P procurements for sourcing teams to benchmark 2-3 vendors even in a renewal scenario, so additional competitors may emerge once the RFP process begins. Dominic was intrigued by the relevant stakeholder/Merlin capabilities at Horizon ('the auto-classification piece is exactly what we need'), which is a wedge against Symphony's legacy analytics. How we win: position iSave + Merlin as the modular, AI-native spend analytics layer purpose-built for post-acquisition supplier rationalization and SAP S/4HANA integration, delivering the tail-spend visibility and auto-categorization Symphony cannot, and make the Merlin contract intelligence agent the Phase 2 centerpiece to extract key terms from Alkermes's legacy contract PDFs. Activate this wedge by co-building a side-by-side Symphony vs Zycus feature/AI comparison with Dominic and securing a live Merlin demo for Tom Harvey and Marcelo Loureiro before the renewal decision cycle begins.

identify_pain.narrative: Dominic Ferrari articulated specific operational pain on the 11 May 2026 call: (1) limited visibility into tail spend and maverick buying with Symphony's current analytics ('we're not getting the insights we need to drive savings in the long tail'), (2) supplier rationalization complexity post-acquisition (Alkermes closed a $2.2B acquisition of an Irish pharma and now faces duplicate suppliers and fragmented contracts across geographies), and (3) manual effort to categorize spend and extract contract terms from legacy PDFs ('our team spends hours every month just trying to clean up spend data and pull out key contract dates'). He also noted that Alkermes is mid-way through a 15-month ERP consolidation (SAP, Oracle, JAGGAER to SAP S/4HANA) and described the pain of integrating disconnected S2P tools into the new ERP ('we need something modular that plugs into S/4 without forcing us to rip out what we've already built'). The pain owner is Dominic's team (Strategic Sourcing under Tom Harvey and Marcelo Loureiro), who are directly responsible for tail spend management and post-acquisition supplier consolidation. The pain is confirmed and well-articulated, but it has not been quantified (no baseline cost, no savings target), and it has not been validated by the pain owner's manager (Tom Harvey) or the Economic Buyer (Ricky Levine), which limits its conversion into a funded initiative.

Dominic mentioned the ERP project is 15 months in and consuming significant staff bandwidth, which could delay legal/IT resourcing for a parallel S2P procurement, but he did not describe the procurement-to-contract handoff process. The Symphony contract expiration date (estimated 'possibly around December' 2026 by Dominic, but unconfirmed) is the only tangible forcing function, but without the actual date the urgency and contract negotiation timeline are ambiguous. The move is to re-engage Dominic and request an intro to the relevant stakeholder/Procurement Ops to understand the standard SaaS procurement workflow, MSA requirements, and InfoSec approval SLA, and to confirm the Symphony renewal notice period (typically 90-120 days) so Zycus can back-plan the contracting timeline.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is partially understood but not buyer-confirmed. Dominic Ferrari described the stakeholder landscape on 11 May: he reports to Tom Harvey (VP Strategic Sourcing), who reports to Marcelo Loureiro (Head of Global Procurement), and budget decisions flow through the new CFO Ricky Levine. Dominic stated 'Marcelo and Tom would need to sign off, and then it goes to Ricky for budget approval', suggesting a three-layer approval chain (Procurement leadership → CFO). He also noted that Alkermes recently consolidated procurement decision-making post-acquisition, centralizing vendor selection under Marcelo's global team, which implies a formal governance process. However, Dominic has not shared a written decision timeline, stage-gate milestones, or procurement policy documents, and he was uncertain about the Symphony contract expiration date ('possibly around December, I'd have to go look'), which is the forcing function for the evaluation. No Legal, InfoSec, or IT stakeholders have been named or engaged, and it is unclear whether the ERP consolidation team (which Dominic described as consuming 'significant staff time') has a formal touchpoint in the S2P vendor approval process. The call sequence shows only Dominic engaged—no escalation to Tom Harvey or Marcelo Loureiro has occurred, signaling the process has not formally started. The move is to re-engage Dominic and request an intro to Tom Harvey to co-develop a realistic evaluation roadmap anchored to the Symphony renewal date, and to map the InfoSec/IT approval gates required for a SaaS procurement.

decision_criteria.narrative: Dominic Ferrari outlined high-level evaluation priorities on the 11 May call: (1) visibility into tail spend and maverick buying, (2) supplier rationalization analytics to support post-acquisition consolidation, (3) modular S2P architecture that integrates with the new SAP S/4HANA ERP without requiring a rip-and-replace, and (4) AI-driven spend categorization and contract intelligence (he was intrigued by Merlin's auto-classification capabilities at Horizon). These are directionally useful but not formalized criteria—Dominic has not shared a BRD, RFP scorecard, or weighted decision matrix, and he acknowledged 'we haven't kicked off a formal evaluation yet, just exploring what's out there'. The criteria are inferred from his articulated pain points, not confirmed as the official evaluation framework the buying committee will use. Without EB/DM engagement it is unclear whether Ricky Levine or Marcelo Loureiro will add financial/procurement-specific criteria (ROI hurdle, contract flexibility, implementation risk). The move is to re-engage Dominic and request a working session to co-build a draft evaluation scorecard that he can socialize with Marcelo and Ricky, positioning Zycus as a consultative partner shaping the process rather than reacting to an RFP.

Alkermes is mid-ERP-consolidation and post-acquisition, focused on operational efficiency and supplier rationalization—AI is a means to that end, not an end in itself. Dominic Ferrari was intrigued by Merlin's contract intelligence at Horizon. However, fit is inferred from one discovery call and has not been validated through a structured demo, use-case workshop, or BRD mapping exercise with the broader buying committee (Tom Harvey, Marcelo Loureiro, Ricky Levine). Integration with SAP S/4HANA is a stated requirement and likely a decision criterion—Zycus's ERP connector capability is strong, but no technical validation or IT stakeholder engagement has occurred. hard.ais_why:
* Warming to agentic AI. On the 11-May discovery, the buyer's stance was that "Initial skepticism about Agentic AI shifted after it dominated post-conference discussions" - growing openness, not yet conviction.
* Interest is hedged by AI-reliability doubts: "Concerns over AI-driven spend analytics data accuracy and lack of transparency in external tools." Value of AI must still be proven before it drives the deal.
* Early and single-threaded: Dominic is the lone internal advocate, JAGGAER is incumbent, and next steps are only a Gartner Magic Quadrant link, an overview deck and a Horizon invite - no POC, budget, or AI-specific evaluation. Engagement is driven by SRM/supplier-diversity needs more than AI pull.

Single-thread risk: Dominic Ferrari is the sole known buyer stakeholder and the only advocate ('right now, you have only one advocate, me'), with zero buyer-side contact roles on the opp and no other Alkermes participants on the 11 May intro call. He has not introduced Zycus to the Economic Buyer (new CFO Ricky Levine), Decision Maker (likely Marcelo Loureiro, Head of Global Procurement), or IT stakeholders. Multi-thread immediately by asking Dominic for introductions to procurement and finance leadership, positioning a spend analytics gap assessment as the reason for a broader stakeholder call.

Champion at risk of fading: Dominic committed to confirm the Symphony expiration date and socialize Zycus materials internally but has not replied in 46 days (as of 26 June 2026). He explicitly warned the rep not to get too excited because internal stakeholders dismissed his Horizon insights ('people just poo pah it'). Re-engage with a low-friction, high-value offer (a free spend analytics gap assessment on Alkermes data, demonstrating classification improvements vs. Symphony) to reignite his advocacy and give him a concrete win to take to his team.

Competing priorities and org bandwidth: Alkermes is 15 months into a large ERP consolidation (SAP, Oracle, JAGGAER to SAP S/4HANA), recently closed a $2.2B acquisition of an Irish pharma, and is navigating FDA leadership uncertainty affecting their narcolepsy drug phase 3 trial. Dominic noted 'you're asking many staff members to dedicate significant amounts of their time' to the ERP project, signaling bandwidth constraints. These factors may delay or deprioritize a spend analytics evaluation despite the Symphony renewal forcing function (~December 2026). The move is to position Zycus as a low-disruption, high-ROI quick win (modular deployment, no ERP rip-and-replace) and anchor the evaluation to the Symphony renewal timeline to create urgency.

Economic Buyer and budget process unmapped: No commercial conversation, no EB identified, no pricing discussed. Dominic is an influencer (Associate Director, Supplier Diversity) but does not control budget. The new CFO (Ricky Levine, hired post-Horizon) is a likely EB, but Dominic has not introduced Zycus to him or to procurement leadership (Marcelo Loureiro, Head of Global Procurement). Map the EB and approval chain by asking Dominic who approves spend analytics tool purchases and secure an introduction to that person, positioning the spend analytics gap assessment as the reason for the meeting.

value.how_we_win: Position Zycus as the integrated, transparent alternative: homogeneous S2P platform (spend analytics + SRM) with superior classification accuracy (no multi-vendor stitching), AI explainability, and supplier diversity certification tracking (a Symphony gap). Offer a live spend analytics assessment on Alkermes data to demonstrate the classification and unclassified-spend improvements vs. Symphony, anchored to the December 2026 renewal timeline. Emphasize dedicated customer success vs. Symphony's support-line model.

value.detail: Organizational bandwidth constraints and competing priorities. Alkermes is 15 months into a large ERP consolidation (SAP, Oracle, JAGGAER to SAP S/4HANA), recently closed a $2.2B acquisition of an Irish pharma, and is navigating FDA leadership uncertainty affecting a phase 3 narcolepsy drug trial. Dominic noted 'you're asking many staff members to dedicate significant amounts of their time' to the ERP project, signaling that a parallel spend analytics evaluation may be deprioritized despite the Symphony renewal forcing function. Risk: deal stalls as internal stakeholders defer S2P decisions until ERP project stabilizes.
value.source: Avoma discovery call 11 May 2026, Dominic Ferrari described ERP consolidation bandwidth constraints and $2.2B acquisition complexity
value.status: Acknowledged but unmitigated. No strategy to position Zycus as low-disruption or phased rollout post-ERP.
